title: encelia canescens var. canescens
intro: Encelia canescens var. canescens, commonly known as the Brittlebrush or Gray Brittlebrush, is a shrubby plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and clusters of small yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of Encelia canescens var. canescens include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate cold and flu symptoms, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, which can be used topically to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
